Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for Julian's own support note about Emilia Cruz's Steinway damage callback request. Tool: conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "callback" (or similar). Finds Julian's message in #customer-support (channel C007) posted on April 13 (ts 1776072420.000000): "Emilia Cruz just called in re: the scratched Steinway from yesterday's move. She was angry and honestly sounded embarrassed about how visible the damage is. Asked for a callback today from someone who can tell her whether MoveOps is taking responsibility or if she has to chase KeyMove herself. I told her we'd get someone back to her today. Leaving this here for pickup." Critical finding: reply_count is 0, latest_reply is null, and reactions_json is empty. Nobody in the channel responded, reacted, or picked up the callback request.
OE 2: Search emails for any outbound MoveOps communication to Emilia Cruz regarding the piano damage or the promised callback. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway callback" or "piano damage" (or similar). The agent should determine whether anyone from MoveOps ever contacted Emilia about the damage after Julian posted the support note. Expected finding: no email was sent from any MoveOps employee to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com about the damage claim status or the callback. This confirms the callback Julian promised on April 13 never happened through any channel.
OE 3: Search emails for the KeyMove vendor damage report describing the Steinway piano scratch. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "KeyMove" or "Steinway" or "Craig Nguyen" or "piano damage" (or similar). Finds email from craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com (CC claims@keymove-specialty.com) addressed to Blessing Okafor at MoveOps. Craig reports on the April 11 delivery for Emilia Cruz: Steinway Model B grand piano, satin ebony, damage on bass side rim near the keyboard cheek block, surface scratch approximately 4.5 to 5 inches with finish disruption but no structural crack observed by crew. Damage occurred during extraction at the second-floor landing at 2147 Humboldt St, Denver when the piano had to be rotated more aggressively than planned to clear the interior railing and wall corner.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for the KeyMove bill related to the Steinway damage claim. Tool: quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "KeyMove" (or similar). Finds bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 from vendor KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), DocNumber KM-44192-ICR, transaction date April 17, due date April 24, total amount $1,200. Line item description: "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction." Account charged: Claims and Remediation Expense (ACC-6185). Balance is $1,200. This is the current financial status of the vendor damage claim.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks invoices for the NorthWind Denver expansion billing. Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "NorthWind" (or similar). Finds invoice inv_northwind_denver_02 (DocNumber INV-2026-0412, dated April 1, due May 1, customer NorthWind Technologies, total $14,700, balance $14,700) with 6 line items: (1) Standard Relocation Package for Raj Patel at $4,500, (2) Hazmat Handling Surcharge for lithium UPS batteries at $1,800, (3) Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol at $1,200 marked PROVISIONAL as a first-time service for MoveOps with actual cost pending vendor confirmation, (4) Standard Relocation Package for Emilia Cruz at $4,500, (5) Specialty Piano Moving Subcontractor for the Steinway at $2,200 marked PROVISIONAL pending subcontractor vendor agreement and insurance verification, and (6) Early Lease Termination Negotiation at $500. Two of the provisional line items correspond to services that subsequently failed: the chain-of-custody protocol was breached and the piano moving resulted in damage.
OE 6: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's internal review of the KeyMove bill and his assessment of the NorthWind cost and retention pattern. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "KeyMove bill" or "insurance claim rider" or "$1,200" (or similar). Finds email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com). Marcus notes the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider aligns with Craig Nguyen's April 11 damage email and Blessing's note that the walkup assessment was insufficient. He flags the pattern: one service failure creating direct cost exposure on the piano move while the Raj Patel chain-of-custody issue is already a relationship risk in the background. Marcus concludes NorthWind is turning into both a margin problem and a retention problem.
OE 7: Search Slack for Chloe Vance's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach. Tool: conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Raj Patel" or "chain-of-custody" or "SentinelChain" (or similar). Finds Chloe's posted timeline: SentinelChain crew arrived at Raj Patel's address at 7:00 AM on April 10; IT/security escort was confirmed in writing for 9:00 AM via dispatch (Fatimah's April 9 confirmation); crew loaded the server lab equipment before the escort arrived between 7:00 and 9:00 AM; shipment was scanned into SentinelChain's South San Francisco holding warehouse at 4:38 PM for overnight storage; outbound release from warehouse at 8:12 AM April 11. All three of Victor Huang's non-negotiable security requirements were violated: equipment handled before escort sign-in, unlogged custody transfer, and interim warehouse storage. Also finds a documentation integrity flag: the Linear DOT compliance ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) is marked state Done but no signed custody log can be located, while the Airtable relocation record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) still shows Status as In Progress. Cross-system contradiction creates an appearance of closure without actual proof of custody.
OE 8: Search emails for Victor Huang's security requirements and post-breach escalation regarding the Raj Patel server lab relocation. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "Victor Huang" or "security incident" or "server lab" or "chain-of-custody" (or similar). Finds two emails from Victor Huang. First: Victor's pre-move requirements email from victor.huang@northwindtech.com (CC pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) to catalina.dubois@moveops.com laying out non-negotiable security requirements for Raj Patel's move: all 4 servers must have full disk encryption verified before packing, NorthWind IT security escort must physically supervise all packing and loading, signed chain-of-custody log required at every transfer point, direct transport only with no interim warehouse storage, and real-time GPS tracking shared with NorthWind security ops. Second: Victor's post-breach escalation email from victor.huang@northwindtech.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com) to chloe.vance@moveops.com with subject "Immediate security incident response required -- Raj Patel server lab relocation." In this email Victor states the move was a direct violation of the handling controls NorthWind set in writing and MoveOps acknowledged. He demands a formal incident response owner, a written timeline covering crew arrival through all handoffs, names of every person who had physical or documented access to the equipment, confirmation of warehouse location and security controls, and explanation for why the move proceeded without the required IT escort. Victor treats this as a security breach and states he will escalate to HR, procurement, and NorthWind executive team if no owner and response plan are provided by end of day.
OE 9: Search emails for Catalina Dubois's April 14 recovery plan promise to Pam Kowalski. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "recovery plan" or "Catalina NorthWind" or "personal responsibility" (or similar). Finds email from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com dated April 14 in which Catalina acknowledges the seriousness of both the Raj Patel handling issue and the Emilia Cruz piano damage, takes personal responsibility, and promises a "comprehensive service recovery plan within the week" covering: (1) a documented review of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown including corrective controls, (2) a clear status update and resolution path on Emilia Cruz's damage claim, and (3) concrete service changes for more reliable coordination going forward. This recovery plan was never delivered, as confirmed by Pam's subsequent escalation to David citing the unfulfilled commitment.
OE 10: Search emails for Pam Kowalski's final-warning escalation to David Chen. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "Pam Kowalski" or "NorthWind evaluating" or "MoveOps can continue" (or similar). Finds email from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com) stating that NorthWind is now evaluating whether MoveOps can continue to support the account. Pam names both incidents: (1) Raj Patel's server lab relocation was handled in breach of chain-of-custody requirements -- equipment loaded before escort arrived and placed into overnight warehouse storage, and (2) Emilia Cruz's piano was damaged during extraction with lack of timely proactive communication afterward. Pam calls out Catalina's April 14 recovery plan commitment that was never fulfilled. She explicitly states NorthWind has had "preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics" as an alternative relocation provider. Demands a concrete retention proposal from David by end of week covering corrective actions, claim resolution, future account coverage, and commercial consideration.
OE 11: Search Slack and emails for David Chen's save-or-lose communications synthesizing the NorthWind crisis. Tool: conversations_search_messages with a query related to "save-or-lose" or "NorthWind retention" (or similar), and/or search_emails with a query related to "NorthWind" or "save-or-lose" (or similar). Finds David's Slack message in #customer-engagement (channel C002): "We need to treat NorthWind as a save-or-lose account effective now." David explicitly lists his working inputs: Pam's note, Victor's breach escalation, Chloe's reconstruction, Julian's support note, Catalina's April 14 promise, and Alejandro's draft pricing model. He states: "Emilia asked for a same-day callback and we let that sit" and "Pam was promised a recovery plan within the week on April 14 and never got one" and "CRM still reads like March-era account notes while the real account condition deteriorated in Slack, inboxes, and side threads." Requests coordinated responses by 2 PM. Also finds David's email to team (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com) with the same synthesis. Also finds David's message in #executive (channel C004) noting Pam's direct escalation mentioning PathFinder and that the account is in active save mode.
OE 12: Search CRM for NorthWind Technologies company record and associated deals. Tool: crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar), followed by crm_search_deals with company_id "company_northwind" (or similar). Finds company_northwind: NorthWind Technologies, Manufacturing Tech industry, Chicago IL, 150 employees, type Customer, domain northwindtech.com. Finds deal deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2: "NorthWind Technologies -- Denver Satellite Office Expansion (2 Relocations)", amount $14,700, dealstage contractSent, company_id company_northwind. Deal description contains full details of both relocations including Raj Patel's server lab with chain-of-custody requirements and Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano with lease overlap.
OE 13: List CRM engagements for NorthWind to find the account recovery context note. Tool: crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_northwind". Finds engagement eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context (engagement_type NOTE, title "NorthWind Technologies -- Denver Expansion Account Recovery (2 Relocations, April 2026)"). Contains extensive account history: NorthWind's $60,000 January 2026 contract for 5 enterprise relocations at $12K each, Q1 execution failures (HVAC malfunction in Austin, misrepresented listing in Phoenix), Pam's escalation to David Chen, Q1 satisfaction survey scores (5/10 overall, 4/10 communication quality, 5/10 service quality), and notes that Pam explicitly stated NorthWind is giving MoveOps a second chance. Lists current risks including chain-of-custody being unprecedented for MoveOps, piano subcontractor having no existing vendor agreement, and provisional invoice line items. Coordinator listed as Blessing Okafor for both moves, Account Manager Catalina Dubois, CE Lead oversight David Chen.
OE 14: Search Linear for the NorthWind retention response tracking ticket. Tool: linear_list_issues with a query related to "NorthWind" or "retention" (or similar). Finds issue linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, title "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1 (Urgent), assigned to David Chen (moveops_david_chen), team team_operations, due date April 24, created April 24. Description references: Victor Huang security breach email re Raj Patel server lab relocation, Chloe Vance reconstructed ops timeline showing escort mismatch and overnight storage, missing custody-log/documentation integrity gap, Julian Brooks support note showing Emilia Cruz requested same-day callback and no owner picked it up, Catalina Dubois April 14 recovery plan commitment that went unfulfilled, stale CRM notes that do not reflect current account severity, and Alejandro's draft retention model.
OE 15: Read comments on the NorthWind retention Linear ticket. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1". Finds multiple comments:
(a) Chloe Vance (linear_comment_fc9fd7990fec, April 11): Ops timeline reconstruction confirming crew arrival at 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM via Fatimah's dispatch email, load proceeded before escort, equipment held overnight at SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse. Conclusion: operational breach against client's stated controls, not a documentation mix-up.
(b) Alejandro Fuentes (linear_comment_b38c11307d3a, April 16): Draft retention concession started -- 12% Q3 discount only if NorthWind commits full Denver expansion volume. Not ready to socialize, has not incorporated final Raj Patel breach exposure or Emilia Cruz claim cost.
(c) David Chen (linear_comment_dd1f4bceee90): Cross-system picture -- NorthWind sees one vendor failing them in two ways: Raj Patel is a control failure (chain-of-custody broken), Emilia Cruz is a care failure (piano damage plus support follow-up did not happen when promised). Risk amplified by: Pam escalating past Catalina, PathFinder mentioned in the background, Catalina's April 14 promise creating an unmet expectation, CRM not reflecting true severity of account state.
OE 16: Search Airtable for NorthWind Technologies Denver relocation records. Tool: airtable_search_records (after discovering the base and table structure via airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables) searching the Relocations table with field_name "Company" and value "NorthWind Technologies" (or similar). Finds two active Denver relocation records:
(a) recRajPatelChicagoDenver: Raj Patel, NorthWind Technologies, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Denver, Move Start Date April 21, Coordinator Blessing Okafor. Special requirements include server lab with 4 rack-mounted servers, lithium UPS batteries (Class 9 hazmat), CISO-mandated chain-of-custody log, IT security escort supervision, no interim warehouse storage, real-time GPS tracking.
(b) recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver: Emilia Cruz, NorthWind Technologies, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Denver, Move Start Date April 14, Coordinator Blessing Okafor. Special requirements include Steinway Model B grand piano (7ft, 480 lbs) requiring specialty piano movers, three-vendor coordination, 27-day lease overlap (Chicago lease ends May 15, Denver needed by April 18).
OE 17: Look up Emilia Cruz's contact information to obtain her email address for direct outreach. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Emilia Cruz" (or similar). Finds Emilia Cruz, email emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com, phone (303) 555-0184, job title Senior Product Marketing Manager, NorthWind Tech, Denver CO. Description notes she reported damage to her Steinway piano during extraction from a second-floor walkup and called support on April 12 asking whether MoveOps would take responsibility.
OE 18 (Write Action): Send an email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com using send_email. Content should cover: Julian acknowledging his direct role in the Emilia Cruz callback gap (he took her call about the Steinway damage on April 13, promised someone would call back that day, posted in #customer-support for pickup, and nobody followed through -- the message received zero replies); what he found about the damage claim status (KeyMove submitted a $1,200 insurance claim rider, Craig Nguyen documented a 4.5-5 inch scratch on the bass side rim during stairwell extraction, Marcus flagged it as both a cost and retention pattern); his awareness of the broader NorthWind situation including the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and Catalina's unfulfilled recovery plan; and how his piece (the dropped callback) fits into David's save-or-lose assessment.
OE 19 (Write Action): Create a CRM engagement on the NorthWind account to log investigation findings using crm_create_engagement. Parameters: engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), body summarizing the investigation findings including the Emilia Cruz callback gap (zero follow-through after Julian's April 13 support note), KeyMove damage claim status ($1,200 insurance claim rider bill KM-44192-ICR), Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach (all three of Victor Huang's requirements violated), Catalina's April 14 recovery plan that was never delivered, Pam's final-warning escalation citing PathFinder, and the current account state. company_ids should include "company_northwind".
OE 20 (Write Action): Add a comment on the NorthWind retention response tracking ticket in Linear using linear_create_comment. Parameters: issueId "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1", body providing Julian's firsthand context on the Emilia Cruz callback gap (he took her call, promised a same-day callback, posted it in #customer-support for pickup, the message got zero responses), the current damage claim status he found (KeyMove $1,200 insurance claim rider, Craig Nguyen's damage report), and acknowledgment that the dropped callback contributed to the pattern of failures Pam cited in her escalation.
OE 21 (Write Action): Send an email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com using send_email. Content should cover: Julian taking personal responsibility for the missed callback (he took her call, promised someone would get back to her that day, and it did not happen), providing a status update on the damage claim (KeyMove has submitted the insurance claim documentation, MoveOps is processing the $1,200 claim rider), and committing to being her direct point of contact going forward so she does not have to chase MoveOps for updates.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack for Julian's own support note about Emilia Cruz's Steinway damage callback request. Tool: conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway" or "callback" (or similar). Finds Julian's message in #customer-support (channel C007) posted on April 13 (ts 1776072420.000000): "Emilia Cruz just called in re: the scratched Steinway from yesterday's move. She was angry and honestly sounded embarrassed about how visible the damage is. Asked for a callback today from someone who can tell her whether MoveOps is taking responsibility or if she has to chase KeyMove herself. I told her we'd get someone back to her today. Leaving this here for pickup." Critical finding: reply_count is 0, latest_reply is null, and reactions_json is empty. Nobody in the channel responded, reacted, or picked up the callback request.
OE 2: Search emails for any outbound MoveOps communication to Emilia Cruz regarding the piano damage or the promised callback. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "Emilia Cruz" or "Steinway callback" or "piano damage" (or similar). The agent should determine whether anyone from MoveOps ever contacted Emilia about the damage after Julian posted the support note. Expected finding: no email was sent from any MoveOps employee to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com about the damage claim status or the callback. This confirms the callback Julian promised on April 13 never happened through any channel.
OE 3: Search emails for the KeyMove vendor damage report describing the Steinway piano scratch. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "KeyMove" or "Steinway" or "Craig Nguyen" or "piano damage" (or similar). Finds email from craig.nguyen@keymove-specialty.com (CC claims@keymove-specialty.com) addressed to Blessing Okafor at MoveOps. Craig reports on the April 11 delivery for Emilia Cruz: Steinway Model B grand piano, satin ebony, damage on bass side rim near the keyboard cheek block, surface scratch approximately 4.5 to 5 inches with finish disruption but no structural crack observed by crew. Damage occurred during extraction at the second-floor landing at 2147 Humboldt St, Denver when the piano had to be rotated more aggressively than planned to clear the interior railing and wall corner.
OE 4: Search QuickBooks for the KeyMove bill related to the Steinway damage claim. Tool: quickbooks_search_bills with criteria related to "KeyMove" (or similar). Finds bill BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 from vendor KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), DocNumber KM-44192-ICR, transaction date April 17, due date April 24, total amount $1,200. Line item description: "Insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction." Account charged: Claims and Remediation Expense (ACC-6185). Balance is $1,200. This is the current financial status of the vendor damage claim.
OE 5: Search QuickBooks invoices for the NorthWind Denver expansion billing. Tool: quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria related to "NorthWind" (or similar). Finds invoice inv_northwind_denver_02 (DocNumber INV-2026-0412, dated April 1, due May 1, customer NorthWind Technologies, total $14,700, balance $14,700) with 6 line items: (1) Standard Relocation Package for Raj Patel at $4,500, (2) Hazmat Handling Surcharge for lithium UPS batteries at $1,800, (3) Chain-of-Custody Security Protocol at $1,200 marked PROVISIONAL as a first-time service for MoveOps with actual cost pending vendor confirmation, (4) Standard Relocation Package for Emilia Cruz at $4,500, (5) Specialty Piano Moving Subcontractor for the Steinway at $2,200 marked PROVISIONAL pending subcontractor vendor agreement and insurance verification, and (6) Early Lease Termination Negotiation at $500. Two of the provisional line items correspond to services that subsequently failed: the chain-of-custody protocol was breached and the piano moving resulted in damage.
OE 6: Search emails for Marcus Thorne's internal review of the KeyMove bill and his assessment of the NorthWind cost and retention pattern. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "KeyMove bill" or "insurance claim rider" or "$1,200" (or similar). Finds email from marcus.thorne@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com). Marcus notes the $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim rider aligns with Craig Nguyen's April 11 damage email and Blessing's note that the walkup assessment was insufficient. He flags the pattern: one service failure creating direct cost exposure on the piano move while the Raj Patel chain-of-custody issue is already a relationship risk in the background. Marcus concludes NorthWind is turning into both a margin problem and a retention problem.
OE 7: Search Slack for Chloe Vance's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach. Tool: conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Raj Patel" or "chain-of-custody" or "SentinelChain" (or similar). Finds Chloe's posted timeline: SentinelChain crew arrived at Raj Patel's address at 7:00 AM on April 10; IT/security escort was confirmed in writing for 9:00 AM via dispatch (Fatimah's April 9 confirmation); crew loaded the server lab equipment before the escort arrived between 7:00 and 9:00 AM; shipment was scanned into SentinelChain's South San Francisco holding warehouse at 4:38 PM for overnight storage; outbound release from warehouse at 8:12 AM April 11. All three of Victor Huang's non-negotiable security requirements were violated: equipment handled before escort sign-in, unlogged custody transfer, and interim warehouse storage. Also finds a documentation integrity flag: the Linear DOT compliance ticket (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) is marked state Done but no signed custody log can be located, while the Airtable relocation record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) still shows Status as In Progress. Cross-system contradiction creates an appearance of closure without actual proof of custody.
OE 8: Search emails for Victor Huang's security requirements and post-breach escalation regarding the Raj Patel server lab relocation. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "Victor Huang" or "security incident" or "server lab" or "chain-of-custody" (or similar). Finds two emails from Victor Huang. First: Victor's pre-move requirements email from victor.huang@northwindtech.com (CC pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com) to catalina.dubois@moveops.com laying out non-negotiable security requirements for Raj Patel's move: all 4 servers must have full disk encryption verified before packing, NorthWind IT security escort must physically supervise all packing and loading, signed chain-of-custody log required at every transfer point, direct transport only with no interim warehouse storage, and real-time GPS tracking shared with NorthWind security ops. Second: Victor's post-breach escalation email from victor.huang@northwindtech.com (CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com) to chloe.vance@moveops.com with subject "Immediate security incident response required -- Raj Patel server lab relocation." In this email Victor states the move was a direct violation of the handling controls NorthWind set in writing and MoveOps acknowledged. He demands a formal incident response owner, a written timeline covering crew arrival through all handoffs, names of every person who had physical or documented access to the equipment, confirmation of warehouse location and security controls, and explanation for why the move proceeded without the required IT escort. Victor treats this as a security breach and states he will escalate to HR, procurement, and NorthWind executive team if no owner and response plan are provided by end of day.
OE 9: Search emails for Catalina Dubois's April 14 recovery plan promise to Pam Kowalski. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "recovery plan" or "Catalina NorthWind" or "personal responsibility" (or similar). Finds email from catalina.dubois@moveops.com to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com dated April 14 in which Catalina acknowledges the seriousness of both the Raj Patel handling issue and the Emilia Cruz piano damage, takes personal responsibility, and promises a "comprehensive service recovery plan within the week" covering: (1) a documented review of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breakdown including corrective controls, (2) a clear status update and resolution path on Emilia Cruz's damage claim, and (3) concrete service changes for more reliable coordination going forward. This recovery plan was never delivered, as confirmed by Pam's subsequent escalation to David citing the unfulfilled commitment.
OE 10: Search emails for Pam Kowalski's final-warning escalation to David Chen. Tool: search_emails with a query related to "Pam Kowalski" or "NorthWind evaluating" or "MoveOps can continue" (or similar). Finds email from pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC victor.huang@northwindtech.com) stating that NorthWind is now evaluating whether MoveOps can continue to support the account. Pam names both incidents: (1) Raj Patel's server lab relocation was handled in breach of chain-of-custody requirements -- equipment loaded before escort arrived and placed into overnight warehouse storage, and (2) Emilia Cruz's piano was damaged during extraction with lack of timely proactive communication afterward. Pam calls out Catalina's April 14 recovery plan commitment that was never fulfilled. She explicitly states NorthWind has had "preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics" as an alternative relocation provider. Demands a concrete retention proposal from David by end of week covering corrective actions, claim resolution, future account coverage, and commercial consideration.
OE 11: Search Slack and emails for David Chen's save-or-lose communications synthesizing the NorthWind crisis. Tool: conversations_search_messages with a query related to "save-or-lose" or "NorthWind retention" (or similar), and/or search_emails with a query related to "NorthWind" or "save-or-lose" (or similar). Finds David's Slack message in #customer-engagement (channel C002): "We need to treat NorthWind as a save-or-lose account effective now." David explicitly lists his working inputs: Pam's note, Victor's breach escalation, Chloe's reconstruction, Julian's support note, Catalina's April 14 promise, and Alejandro's draft pricing model. He states: "Emilia asked for a same-day callback and we let that sit" and "Pam was promised a recovery plan within the week on April 14 and never got one" and "CRM still reads like March-era account notes while the real account condition deteriorated in Slack, inboxes, and side threads." Requests coordinated responses by 2 PM. Also finds David's email to team (CC elena.rostova@moveops.com) with the same synthesis. Also finds David's message in #executive (channel C004) noting Pam's direct escalation mentioning PathFinder and that the account is in active save mode.
OE 12: Search CRM for NorthWind Technologies company record and associated deals. Tool: crm_search_companies with name "NorthWind" (or similar), followed by crm_search_deals with company_id "company_northwind" (or similar). Finds company_northwind: NorthWind Technologies, Manufacturing Tech industry, Chicago IL, 150 employees, type Customer, domain northwindtech.com. Finds deal deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2: "NorthWind Technologies -- Denver Satellite Office Expansion (2 Relocations)", amount $14,700, dealstage contractSent, company_id company_northwind. Deal description contains full details of both relocations including Raj Patel's server lab with chain-of-custody requirements and Emilia Cruz's Steinway piano with lease overlap.
OE 13: List CRM engagements for NorthWind to find the account recovery context note. Tool: crm_list_engagements with company_ids including "company_northwind". Finds engagement eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context (engagement_type NOTE, title "NorthWind Technologies -- Denver Expansion Account Recovery (2 Relocations, April 2026)"). Contains extensive account history: NorthWind's $60,000 January 2026 contract for 5 enterprise relocations at $12K each, Q1 execution failures (HVAC malfunction in Austin, misrepresented listing in Phoenix), Pam's escalation to David Chen, Q1 satisfaction survey scores (5/10 overall, 4/10 communication quality, 5/10 service quality), and notes that Pam explicitly stated NorthWind is giving MoveOps a second chance. Lists current risks including chain-of-custody being unprecedented for MoveOps, piano subcontractor having no existing vendor agreement, and provisional invoice line items. Coordinator listed as Blessing Okafor for both moves, Account Manager Catalina Dubois, CE Lead oversight David Chen.
OE 14: Search Linear for the NorthWind retention response tracking ticket. Tool: linear_list_issues with a query related to "NorthWind" or "retention" (or similar). Finds issue linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, title "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1 (Urgent), assigned to David Chen (moveops_david_chen), team team_operations, due date April 24, created April 24. Description references: Victor Huang security breach email re Raj Patel server lab relocation, Chloe Vance reconstructed ops timeline showing escort mismatch and overnight storage, missing custody-log/documentation integrity gap, Julian Brooks support note showing Emilia Cruz requested same-day callback and no owner picked it up, Catalina Dubois April 14 recovery plan commitment that went unfulfilled, stale CRM notes that do not reflect current account severity, and Alejandro's draft retention model.
OE 15: Read comments on the NorthWind retention Linear ticket. Tool: linear_list_comments with issueId "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1". Finds multiple comments:
(a) Chloe Vance (linear_comment_fc9fd7990fec, April 11): Ops timeline reconstruction confirming crew arrival at 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM via Fatimah's dispatch email, load proceeded before escort, equipment held overnight at SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse. Conclusion: operational breach against client's stated controls, not a documentation mix-up.
(b) Alejandro Fuentes (linear_comment_b38c11307d3a, April 16): Draft retention concession started -- 12% Q3 discount only if NorthWind commits full Denver expansion volume. Not ready to socialize, has not incorporated final Raj Patel breach exposure or Emilia Cruz claim cost.
(c) David Chen (linear_comment_dd1f4bceee90): Cross-system picture -- NorthWind sees one vendor failing them in two ways: Raj Patel is a control failure (chain-of-custody broken), Emilia Cruz is a care failure (piano damage plus support follow-up did not happen when promised). Risk amplified by: Pam escalating past Catalina, PathFinder mentioned in the background, Catalina's April 14 promise creating an unmet expectation, CRM not reflecting true severity of account state.
OE 16: Search Airtable for NorthWind Technologies Denver relocation records. Tool: airtable_search_records (after discovering the base and table structure via airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables) searching the Relocations table with field_name "Company" and value "NorthWind Technologies" (or similar). Finds two active Denver relocation records:
(a) recRajPatelChicagoDenver: Raj Patel, NorthWind Technologies, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Denver, Move Start Date April 21, Coordinator Blessing Okafor. Special requirements include server lab with 4 rack-mounted servers, lithium UPS batteries (Class 9 hazmat), CISO-mandated chain-of-custody log, IT security escort supervision, no interim warehouse storage, real-time GPS tracking.
(b) recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver: Emilia Cruz, NorthWind Technologies, Status In Progress, Origin Chicago, Destination Denver, Move Start Date April 14, Coordinator Blessing Okafor. Special requirements include Steinway Model B grand piano (7ft, 480 lbs) requiring specialty piano movers, three-vendor coordination, 27-day lease overlap (Chicago lease ends May 15, Denver needed by April 18).
OE 17: Look up Emilia Cruz's contact information to obtain her email address for direct outreach. Tool: contacts_search_contacts with query "Emilia Cruz" (or similar). Finds Emilia Cruz, email emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com, phone (303) 555-0184, job title Senior Product Marketing Manager, NorthWind Tech, Denver CO. Description notes she reported damage to her Steinway piano during extraction from a second-floor walkup and called support on April 12 asking whether MoveOps would take responsibility.
OE 18 (Write Action): Send an email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com using send_email. Content should cover: Julian acknowledging his direct role in the Emilia Cruz callback gap (he took her call about the Steinway damage on April 13, promised someone would call back that day, posted in #customer-support for pickup, and nobody followed through -- the message received zero replies); what he found about the damage claim status (KeyMove submitted a $1,200 insurance claim rider, Craig Nguyen documented a 4.5-5 inch scratch on the bass side rim during stairwell extraction, Marcus flagged it as both a cost and retention pattern); his awareness of the broader NorthWind situation including the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and Catalina's unfulfilled recovery plan; and how his piece (the dropped callback) fits into David's save-or-lose assessment.
OE 19 (Write Action): Create a CRM engagement on the NorthWind account to log investigation findings using crm_create_engagement. Parameters: engagement_type "NOTE" (or similar), body summarizing the investigation findings including the Emilia Cruz callback gap (zero follow-through after Julian's April 13 support note), KeyMove damage claim status ($1,200 insurance claim rider bill KM-44192-ICR), Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach (all three of Victor Huang's requirements violated), Catalina's April 14 recovery plan that was never delivered, Pam's final-warning escalation citing PathFinder, and the current account state. company_ids should include "company_northwind".
OE 20 (Write Action): Add a comment on the NorthWind retention response tracking ticket in Linear using linear_create_comment. Parameters: issueId "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1", body providing Julian's firsthand context on the Emilia Cruz callback gap (he took her call, promised a same-day callback, posted it in #customer-support for pickup, the message got zero responses), the current damage claim status he found (KeyMove $1,200 insurance claim rider, Craig Nguyen's damage report), and acknowledgment that the dropped callback contributed to the pattern of failures Pam cited in her escalation.
OE 21 (Write Action): Send an email from julian.brooks@moveops.com to emilia.cruz@northwindtech.com using send_email. Content should cover: Julian taking personal responsibility for the missed callback (he took her call, promised someone would get back to her that day, and it did not happen), providing a status update on the damage claim (KeyMove has submitted the insurance claim documentation, MoveOps is processing the $1,200 claim rider), and committing to being her direct point of contact going forward so she does not have to chase MoveOps for updates.

You shouldn't have had to wonder whether anyone was working on this, and I take responsibility for not making sure someone followed through after our conversation.'
✓Run #2The email to Emilia explicitly states: 'I told you someone from our team would call you back that same day with answers. That did not happen, and the responsibility for that starts with me.' This directly acknowledges the missed callback and takes personal responsibility.
✓Run #3The email to Emilia states: 'I told you that day that someone from our team would call you back with a clear answer... That callback didn't happen, and I owe you an apology for that. You should not have had to wonder where things stood or chase anyone for an answer.' This clearly acknowledges the missed callback and takes personal responsibility.
✓Run #4The email to Emilia explicitly says: 'I don't believe that callback happened on time, and I'm sorry. You should not have had to chase us for accountability on this. That's not the level of service you were promised, and the delay in follow-through is on me.'
✓Run #5The email to Emilia explicitly states: 'I told you someone from our team would call you back that same day... I am not confident that happened, and if it didn't, I owe you an apology. That was my commitment to you and it was my responsibility to make sure it was followed through.' Clear personal acknowledgment of the missed callback.
✓Run #6The email to Emilia explicitly states: 'I don't believe anyone followed through with that callback. That is not acceptable, and I owe you an apology...The callback commitment was mine to make sure happened, and I let it fall through. I'm sorry.'

24 The model must pass a query related to 'KeyMove' or 'Steinway' or 'piano' or 'insurance claim' (or similar) when calling quickbooks_search_bills to find the damage claim bill.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✗✗✓ 67%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_bills call with criteria [['VendorRef.name', 'LIKE', '%KeyMove%']], which directly references KeyMove as required by the criterion. This was the first attempt; a second fetchAll call also occurred after the first errored.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_bills call with criteria filtering on VendorRef.name LIKE '%KeyMove%', directly referencing the KeyMove vendor name to find the damage claim bill.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a quickbooks_search_bills call with criteria [['VendorRef.name', 'LIKE', '%KeyMove%']], which directly references the vendor name 'KeyMove'. Even though it returned an error, the query was correctly constructed with KeyMove-related terms as required by the criterion.
✗Run #4The agent never called quickbooks_search_bills at all. It called quickbooks_search_invoices with criteria {CustomerRef: 'NorthWind'} — not using KeyMove, Steinway, piano, or insurance claim as search terms. The criterion requires a quickbooks_search_bills call with vendor/damage-related terms, which did not occur.
✗Run #5The agent called quickbooks_search_invoices (not quickbooks_search_bills) with criteria {CustomerRef: 'NorthWind'}. The agent never called quickbooks_search_bills at all, let alone with KeyMove, Steinway, piano, or insurance claim as the query. The KeyMove insurance claim rider is a vendor bill, not a customer invoice, so the agent used the wrong tool and wrong query terms.
✓Run #6The first quickbooks_search_bills call used criteria: [['VendorRef.name', 'LIKE', '%KeyMove%']], which explicitly references 'KeyMove' — one of the required search terms. This satisfies the criterion.
